title: Galatella fominii
taxonomy: Galatella fominii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the genus Galatella, which consists of various species of daisy-like plants. Galatella fominii is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia, specifically in Kyrgyzstan and Tajikistan. The plant is a perennial herb, characterized by its bright yellow or white flowers and a compact growth habit. Galatella fominii is often found growing in mountain meadows and rocky outcrops at high elevations.

intro: Galatella fominii, also known as Fomin's galatella, is a perennial plant belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, wounds, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Galatella fominii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects.
